---
title: "Data Handling"
subtitle: "Filter rows, pick columns, sort, and reshape your data with dplyr."
toc: false
---

<details class="topic-card" open>
<summary>Setup</summary>
<div class="topic-card-body">

Run this setup chunk first — it loads the dataset used in all examples below.

```{webr-r}
library(tidyverse)
#library(dplyr)
#library(tibble)

# Load from mounted webR paths first; fallback to downloading from site URLs.
load_capsules <- function() {
  candidate_paths <- c(
    "/home/web_user/capsules.csv",
    "capsules.csv",
    "assets/data/capsules.csv",
    "../assets/data/capsules.csv",
    "/assets/data/capsules.csv"
  )

  for (path in candidate_paths) {
    out <- tryCatch(
      read_csv(path, show_col_types = FALSE),
      error = function(e) NULL
    )
    if (!is.null(out)) return(out)
  }

  candidate_urls <- c(
    "../assets/data/capsules.csv",
    "/assets/data/capsules.csv"
  )

  for (url in candidate_urls) {
    out <- tryCatch({
      tmp <- tempfile(fileext = ".csv")
      utils::download.file(url, destfile = tmp, mode = "wb", quiet = TRUE)
      read_csv(tmp, show_col_types = FALSE)
    }, error = function(e) NULL)
    if (!is.null(out)) return(out)
  }

  stop(
    "Could not load capsules.csv from webR mounted paths or site asset URLs. "
  )
}

capsules <- load_capsules()

capsules
```

</div>
</details>

<details class="topic-card">
<summary>filter( )</summary>
<div class="topic-card-body">

Keep only rows where a condition is `TRUE`.

```{webr-r}
# Keep only batch A capsules
capsules %>%
  filter(batch == "A")
```

```{webr-r}
# Keep rows matching two conditions
capsules %>%
  filter(batch == "B" & pct_label > 100)
```

::: {.callout-tip}
## Comparison operators
`==` equal · `!=` not equal · `>` `<` `>=` `<=` greater/less than · `&` and · `|` or
:::

</div>
</details>

<details class="topic-card">
<summary>select( )</summary>
<div class="topic-card-body">

Keep only the columns you need.

```{webr-r}
capsules %>%
  select(capsule_id, batch, pct_label)
```

```{webr-r}
# Drop a column with -
capsules %>%
  select(-fill_mass_mg)
```

</div>
</details>

<details class="topic-card">
<summary>rename( )</summary>
<div class="topic-card-body">

Rename a column: `rename(new_name = old_name)`.

```{webr-r}
capsules %>%
  rename(id = capsule_id, pct = pct_label)
```

</div>
</details>

<details class="topic-card">
<summary>arrange( )</summary>
<div class="topic-card-body">

Sort rows by a column. Wrap in `desc()` to sort highest-first.

```{webr-r}
capsules %>%
  arrange(desc(pct_label))
```

</div>
</details>

<details class="topic-card">
<summary>mutate( )</summary>
<div class="topic-card-body">

Add or transform columns. See the full [`mutate()` reference page](../functions/mutate.qmd) for details.

```{webr-r}
label_claim_mg <- 75.0

capsules %>%
  mutate(ibu_mg = fill_mass_mg * 0.43) %>%
  select(capsule_id, fill_mass_mg, ibu_mg, pct_label)
```

</div>
</details>

<details class="topic-card">
<summary>Combining steps</summary>
<div class="topic-card-body">

Chain any number of operations with the pipe — each result flows into the next.

```{webr-r}
capsules %>%
  filter(batch == "B") %>%
  arrange(pct_label) %>%
  select(capsule_id, fill_mass_mg, pct_label)
```

</div>
</details>
